mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month average, or the signal shows no predictive power during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates persistent price pressures that force continued central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals economic contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters with negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to predict returns during historical recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in industrial production and housing starts indicates a broad-based slowdown in economic activity, prompting immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when both series decline simultaneously, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
